sections.paragraph:
(each Tzere impure) a poetic word-

(1) fertile and inhabited earth, the habitable globe, οἰκουμένη (from the root יָבַל, of which see Hiphil No. 3, Syr. ܬܶܙܒܶܠ, ܬܶܒܙܶܠ, ܬܒܝܠ id.), Isa 14:17 (opp. to מִדְבָּר). There twice occurs poetically תֵּבֵל אַרְצֹו the world of his (God’s) earth, Pro 8:31 Job 37:12 compare אֶרֶץ וְתֵבֵל Psal. 90:2 . It often denotes-

(2) the whole earth, especially where the creation of the world is mentioned, 1Sa 2:8 Psa 18:16 93:1 meton. the inhabitants of the earth, Psa 9:9 24:1 33:8 96:13 98:9 . Hyperbolically applied to the kingdom of Babylon, Isa 13:11 to that of Israel, Isa 24:4 (compare orbis Romanus). [Whatever be the meaning of תֵּבֵל in these passages, no one who believes in the inspiration of Scripture can admit that they contain real hyperbole.]
